title: wrong result for GROUP BY ROLLUP
description: ### What happens? It appears that under certain concurrency levels, when DISTINCT is used together with GROUP BY ROLLUP, it may lead to incorrect results. ### To Reproduce ```sql create table test(col1 int, col2 int, col3 int); insert into test values(22,6,8),(28,57,45),(82,44,71); set threads=4; select distinct * from test group by rollup (col3,col2,col1,col1); ``` You can run the fourth query from the reproduction steps above multiple times to obtain results similar to those shown in the following figure. Regarding the cause, I suspect it might be related to duplicate columns after GROUP BY ROLLUP. However, in any case, I consider the following results to be an error. ### OS: x86_64 ### DuckDB Version: 1.3.2 ### DuckDB Client: CLI ### Hardware: _No response_ ### Full Name: Amore ### Affiliation: Software engineer ### Did you include all relevant configuration (e.g., CPU architecture, Linux distribution) to reproduce the issue? - [x] Yes, I have ### Did you include all code required to reproduce the issue? - [x] Yes, I have ### Did you include all relevant data sets for reproducing the issue? Yes
poc: create table test(col1 int, col2 int, col3 int);
select distinct * from test group by rollup (col3,col2,col1,col1);
key_bug_features: DISTINCT with GROUP BY ROLLUP under concurrency
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE test_v2(col1 INT, col2 INT, col3 INT, col4 VARCHAR(10) NOT NULL);
SELECT DISTINCT col4, col3 FROM test_v2 GROUP BY ROLLUP(col4, col3);
CREATE TABLE test_v3(col1 FLOAT, col2 TEXT, col3 BOOLEAN);
INSERT INTO test_v3 VALUES(22.5, 'text1', TRUE), (28.3, 'text2', FALSE), (NULL, 'text3', TRUE);
